campaign: BC4 round-15 timed silicon wall — sm_90 async warpgroup MMA (wgmma.mma_async)
verdict_tier: RED — hardware-blocked (honest closed-negative; rule-out one axis per paper_negative_ok)
host: ubu-2 RTX 5070 sm_120 Blackwell (driver 580.159.03, nvcc 12.9.86 + nvcc 12.0)
arch_supported_reason: wgmma is Hopper-exclusive (sm_90a). RTX 5070 = Blackwell = sm_120. ptxas-12.9 explicitly rejects: 'wgmma.mma_async with floating point types cannot be compiled for architecture sm_120'. Blackwell's analogous async tensor-core path is tcgen05.mma — a different instruction family with different operand layout (5th-gen tensor cores), not a wgmma drop-in.

probes.sm90a_build: PASS — nvcc 12.9 -gencode arch=compute_90a,code=sm_90a compiles wgmma.mma_async.sync.aligned.m64n32k16.f32.f16.f16 cleanly. ptxas 42 registers, 0 spill, 1 barrier. SASS confirms HGMMA.64x32x16.F32 instruction in cubin.
probes.sm90a_driver_jit_to_sm120: RUNS but SILENTLY NOPs — kernel launches OK (no CUDA_ERROR_NO_BINARY_FOR_GPU 209) but ALL outputs zero. CPU reference sum=1956.87, GPU sum=0.00, nonzero=0/2048 floats. Driver forward-compat from sm_90 PTX to sm_120 SASS does NOT translate wgmma → Blackwell tensor-core ops.
probes.sm120_build: FAIL — ptxas explicitly: 'wgmma.mma_async with floating point types cannot be compiled for architecture sm_120' (also wgmma.commit_group + wgmma.wait_group rejected). Confirms wgmma is NOT part of Blackwell PTX surface.

ptxas_v_sm90a.verdict: ptxas clean — no spill, no stack — kernel itself is well-formed

capstone_status: CANNOT-TEST on ubu-2 RTX 5070. Would require Hopper hardware (H100/H200/H800 = sm_90) to attempt. Without Hopper access, BC4 R14 (BM=32 BK=32, 0.927× at N=4096) remains capstone.
ruled_out_axis: wgmma capstone wedge — the ONE remaining attention lever R11 had ruled-out / not-tried — is now CONFIRMED-RULED-OUT on RTX 5070 (Blackwell). Not because the wedge is bad, but because the host hardware is wrong generation for the instruction.
honest_note: Following paper_negative_ok + g3 over-claim 0: this is a publishable closed-negative finding — wgmma cannot be tested on Blackwell. The pre-registered falsifier F-FUSION-ATTN-WGMMA-WALL collapses to 'hardware-blocked, NOT instruction-failed'. R11's note that wgmma was 'ruled-out / not-tried' was correct in spirit but the ruling-out reason was hardware unavailability, not algorithmic. R14 capstone (PR #1735) stands as the BC4 attention closure.

compute_capability_mapping.Ada Lovelace (RTX 40xx): sm_89 — wgmma NOT supported (uses standard mma.sync)
compute_capability_mapping.Blackwell (RTX 50xx / B100/B200): sm_120 / sm_100 — tcgen05.mma INSTEAD of wgmma (different opcode, different operand layout, 5th-gen tensor cores)

next_lever_in_cycle_all: Pivot to FP8 GEMM (item 5 in /cycle-all queue) — also a tensor-core acceleration path but uses .e4m3/.e5m2 inputs through mma.sync.aligned (NOT wgmma). FP8 is supported on sm_89+ (Ada/Hopper/Blackwell) including RTX 5070. Alternative future path: tcgen05.mma for Blackwell-native async warpgroup MMA (would require ubu-2-specific re-investigation as analogous capstone-extension).
preserves: g3 over-claim 0 — honest RED tier per measurement, not faked PASS
paper_negative_ok — closed-negative is publishable: rules-out wgmma-on-Blackwell axis deterministically
feedback_closure_is_physical_limit — physical limit here is hardware-instruction-incompatibility (Hopper vs Blackwell), not algorithmic ceiling
